title: Agatha Christie — Murder on the Orient Express

intro.title: The Calais Coach
intro.body: Agatha Christie travelled the Orient Express repeatedly between 1928 and 1932 to join her husband Max Mallowan at the Ur excavation. A train snowed in at Çerkezköy in February 1929 furnished the setting for Murder on the Orient Express (1934).

stops.name: London (Victoria)
stops.note: Christie boards the Calais boat-train at Victoria Station with her travelling typewriter.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia hand-coloured plate, late-1920s register. Victoria station with porters in caps, Pullman carriage of the Wagons-Lits, lady novelist with hatbox.

stops.name: Calais
stops.note: Cross-Channel ferry, then the Calais-Mediterranée Express on the French rails.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia hand-coloured plate. Channel ferry at French quay, Wagons-Lits express drawn up beside the gangway, porters trundling luggage.

stops.name: Belgrade
stops.note: Wagon-restaurant stop in the Balkans. By 1932 the Simplon route replaces the original; Christie usually takes the Simplon line.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia hand-coloured plate. Snowy Belgrade station at evening, dining car with white-clothed tables glimpsed through windows.

stops.name: Çerkezköy
stops.note: Thracian village. The 1929 blizzard stranded Christie's train here for six days — the seed of the novel.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia hand-coloured plate. Snowed-in train on a curved cutting in flat Thracian countryside, single lit window, village in the distance.

stops.name: Istanbul (Sirkeci)
stops.note: Sirkeci terminus on the Bosphorus. Christie crosses by ferry to the Asian shore for the onward Taurus Express to Mosul.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia hand-coloured plate. Sirkeci station seafront, ferry boats puffing across the Golden Horn, lady writer in cloche hat on the steps.
